papers.title: OScaR: The Occam's Razor for Extreme KV Cache Quantization in LLMs and Beyond
papers.abstract: Abstract OScaR is a novel KV cache compression framework that addresses token norm imbalance through canalized rotation and omni-token scaling, achieving significant improvements in memory efficiency and decoding speed for extended context language models.  AI-generated summary The rapid advancement toward long-context reasoning and multi-modal intelligence has made the memory footprint of the Key-Value (KV) cache a dominant memory bottleneck for efficient deployment. While the established per-channel quantization effectively accommodates intrinsic channel-wise outliers in Key tensors, its efficacy diminishes under extreme compression. In this work, we revisit the inherent limitations of the per-channel quantization paradigm from both empirical and theoretical perspectives. Our analysis identifies Token Norm Imbalance (TNI) as the primary bottleneck to quantization fidelity. We demonstrate that TNI systematically amplifies errors when shared quantization parameters are required to span token groups exhibiting substantial norm disparities. Instead of relying on intricate quantization pipelines (e.g., TurboQuant), we propose OScaR (Omni-Scaled Canalized Rotation), an accurate and lightweight KV cache compression framework for X-LLMs (i.e., text-only, multi-modal, and omni-modal LLMs). Advancing the per-channel paradigm, OScaR employs Canalized Rotation followed by Omni-Token Scaling to mitigate TNI-induced sequence-dimensional variance both effectively and efficiently, further supported by our optimized system design and CUDA kernels. Extensive evaluations across X-LLMs show that OScaR consistently outperforms existing methods and achieves near-lossless performance under INT2 quantization, establishing it as a robust, low-complexity, and universal framework that defines a new Pareto front. Compared with the BF16 FlashDecoding-v2 baseline, our OScaR implementation achieves a notable up to 3.0x speedup in decoding, reduces memory footprint by 5.3x, and increases throughput by 4.1x. The code for OScaR is publicly available at https://github.com/ZunhaiSu/OScaR-KV-Quant.

ru.title: Сбалансированное сжатие KV кэша для быстрого декодирования больших языковых моделей
ru.desc: OScaR — это новая система сжатия KV кэша, которая решает проблему дисбаланса норм токенов через канализированное вращение и масштабирование всех токенов. Авторы показали, что стандартная per-channel квантизация неэффективна при сильном сжатии, так как разные токены имеют сильно различающиеся нормы. Предложенный метод применяет трансформацию координат и адаптивное масштабирование, чтобы уменьшить дисперсию норм токенов и повысить точность квантизации. Экспериментальные результаты демонстрируют, что OScaR достигает ускорения декодирования в 3 раза, снижает использование памяти в 5,3 раза и увеличивает пропускную способность в 4,1 раза при INT2 квантизации.

en.title: Revolutionizing KV Cache Compression with OScaR
en.desc: OScaR is a new framework designed to improve the efficiency of Key-Value (KV) cache compression in language models. It tackles the issue of Token Norm Imbalance (TNI), which can lead to errors in quantization when different tokens have varying magnitudes. By using techniques like Canalized Rotation and Omni-Token Scaling, OScaR enhances memory efficiency and speeds up decoding processes. The framework has shown significant performance improvements, achieving up to 3.0x faster decoding and reducing memory usage by 5.3x compared to previous methods.

zh.title: OScaR：高效的KV缓存压缩框架
zh.desc: OScaR是一种新颖的键值（KV）缓存压缩框架，旨在通过通道旋转和全通道缩放来解决令牌范数不平衡问题，从而显著提高扩展上下文语言模型的内存效率和解码速度。该研究指出，传统的每通道量化在极端压缩下效果减弱，主要瓶颈在于令牌范数不平衡（TNI），这会在共享量化参数时放大误差。OScaR通过通道旋转和全通道缩放有效缓解了TNI引起的序列维度方差，展现出优越的性能。与现有方法相比，OScaR在INT2量化下实现了接近无损的性能，解码速度提高了3.0倍，内存占用减少了5.3倍，吞吐量增加了4.1倍。

papers.title: It Takes Two: Complementary Self-Distillation for Contextual Integrity in LLMs
papers.abstract: Abstract SELFCI is a self-distillation framework that separates information suppression from task resolution to achieve better privacy-utility balance in large language models without external supervision.  AI-generated summary Contextual Integrity (CI) defines privacy not merely as keeping information hidden, but as governing information flows according to the norms of a given context. As large language models are increasingly deployed as personal agents handling sensitive workflows, adhering to CI becomes critical. However, even frontier models remain unreliable in making disclosure decisions, and existing mitigation strategies often degrade underlying task performance. To overcome this privacy-utility trade-off, we propose SELFCI, a complementary self-distillation framework that decouples information suppression from task resolution. SELFCI jointly optimizes two independent reverse KL divergences over distinct teacher distributions derived from feedback: one encourages preserving task-relevant information for utility, while the other enforces minimal and appropriate disclosure. This complementary formulation induces a Product-of-Experts (PoE) target, aligning the policy with the intersection of capability and privacy requirements. Empirical evaluations demonstrate that SELFCI, without relying on costly external supervision, consistently outperforms competitive baselines such as online reinforcement learning algorithms (e.g., GRPO). These trends further extend to out-of-domain settings involving agentic workflows and accumulated private context, suggesting that SELFCI provides a practical path toward CI alignment.

ru.title: Баланс приватности и функциональности через самодистилляцию без внешнего надзора
ru.desc: SELFCI — это фреймворк самодистилляции, который разделяет подавление информации и решение основной задачи для достижения лучшего баланса между конфиденциальностью и полезностью больших языковых моделей. Подход основан на принципах контекстной целостности, которые определяют приватность не просто как скрытие информации, а как управление информационными потоками согласно нормам контекста. Метод оптимизирует две независимые обратные KL-дивергенции над различными распределениями учителей, побуждая модель сохранять релевантную для задачи информацию и одновременно минимизировать неправильное раскрытие чувствительных данных. Экспериментальная оценка показывает, что SELFCI превосходит конкурентные подходы на основе обучения с подкреплением без требования дорогостоящего внешнего контроля.

en.title: Balancing Privacy and Utility in AI with SELFCI
en.desc: SELFCI is a self-distillation framework designed to improve the balance between privacy and utility in large language models. It separates the processes of information suppression and task resolution, allowing models to better manage sensitive information without external supervision. By optimizing two independent reverse KL divergences, SELFCI ensures that task-relevant information is preserved while minimizing unnecessary disclosures. Empirical results show that SELFCI outperforms existing methods, making it a promising approach for aligning AI systems with Contextual Integrity principles.

zh.title: 隐私与效用的完美平衡
zh.desc: SELFCI是一种自我蒸馏框架，旨在在大型语言模型中实现更好的隐私与效用平衡，而无需外部监督。该框架通过将信息抑制与任务解决分离，优化两个独立的反向KL散度，以保持任务相关信息并最小化适当的信息披露。通过这种互补的形式，SELFCI能够在能力与隐私要求的交集上对齐策略。实证评估表明，SELFCI在没有昂贵外部监督的情况下，始终优于竞争基线，如在线强化学习算法。

papers.title: You Only Need Minimal RLVR Training: Extrapolating LLMs via Rank-1 Trajectories
papers.abstract: Abstract Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards parameter trajectories exhibit low-rank structures that enable efficient extrapolation through a simple linear regression method, demonstrating superior performance with reduced computational requirements.  AI-generated summary Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has become a dominant paradigm for improving reasoning in large language models (LLMs), yet the underlying geometry of the resulting parameter trajectories remains underexplored. In this work, we demonstrate that RLVR weight trajectories are extremely low-rank and highly predictable. Specifically, we find that the majority of downstream performance gains are captured by a rank-1 approximation of the parameter deltas, where the magnitude of this projection evolves near-linearly with training steps. Motivated by this, we propose a simple and compute-efficient method RELEX (REinforcement Learning EXtrapolation), which estimates the rank-1 subspace from a short observation window and extrapolates future checkpoints via linear regression, with no learned model required. Across three models (i.e., Qwen2.5-Math-1.5B, Qwen3-4B-Base, and Qwen3-8B-Base), RELEX produces checkpoints that match or exceed RLVR performance on both in-domain and out-of-domain benchmarks, requiring as few as 15% steps of full RLVR training. Remarkably, RELEX is able to extrapolate far beyond the observation window at no training cost, predicting checkpoints up to 10-20times beyond the observed prefix with continued improvement (e.g., observe only the first 50 steps and extrapolate to 1000 steps). Our ablation analysis confirms the minimalist sufficiency of RELEX: neither increasing the subspace rank nor employing non-linear modeling yields further gains in extrapolation. Finally, we show that RELEX's success stems from a "denoising" effect: by projecting updates onto the rank-1 subspace, the model discards stochastic optimization noise that would otherwise degrade performance during extrapolation. Our code is available at https://github.com/weizhepei/RELEX.

ru.title: Линейная экстраполяция обучения с подкреплением через низкоранговую структуру
ru.desc: Авторы исследуют структуру траекторий параметров в обучении с подкреплением и проверяемыми наградами (RLVR), используемом для улучшения рассуждений в больших языковых моделях. Они обнаруживают, что эти траектории обладают низкоранговой структурой, где большая часть улучшений производительности объясняется одномерной аппроксимацией. На основе этого предлагается метод RELEX, который предсказывает будущие контрольные точки модели через линейную регрессию, требуя всего 15% шагов полного обучения. Метод способен экстраполировать на 10-20 раз дальше наблюдаемого окна, отфильтровывая стохастический шум оптимизации через проецирование на низкоранговое подпространство.

en.title: Efficient Extrapolation in Reinforcement Learning with RELEX
en.desc: This paper explores a new method called RELEX for reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR), which focuses on the low-rank structure of parameter trajectories. The authors find that most performance improvements can be captured using a simple rank-1 approximation of the parameter changes, allowing for efficient predictions. RELEX uses linear regression to extrapolate future model checkpoints from a short observation window, significantly reducing computational costs while maintaining or improving performance. The method effectively filters out noise from the optimization process, leading to better extrapolation results without the need for complex models.

zh.title: 低秩外推：RELEX的高效强化学习方法
zh.desc: 本研究探讨了可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）中参数轨迹的低秩结构，这种结构使得通过简单的线性回归方法进行高效外推成为可能。我们发现，RLVR的权重轨迹具有极低的秩，并且高度可预测，主要的性能提升可以通过对参数增量的秩-1近似来捕捉。基于此，我们提出了一种简单且计算高效的方法RELEX，它通过短时间观察窗口估计秩-1子空间，并通过线性回归外推未来的检查点。RELEX在多个模型上表现出色，能够在仅需15%的RLVR训练步骤下，预测出超出观察窗口的检查点，且性能优于传统方法。

papers.title: Video2GUI: Synthesizing Large-Scale Interaction Trajectories for Generalized GUI Agent Pretraining
papers.abstract: Abstract A large-scale GUI dataset was created by automatically extracting interaction trajectories from internet videos, enabling improved performance in GUI agents through pre-training on this diverse collection.  AI-generated summary Recent advances in multimodal large language models have driven growing interest in graphical user interface (GUI) agents, yet their generalization remains constrained by the scarcity of large-scale training data spanning diverse real-world applications. Existing datasets rely heavily on costly manual annotations and are typically confined to narrow domains. To address this challenge, we propose Video2GUI, a fully automated framework that extracts grounded GUI interaction trajectories directly from unlabeled Internet videos. Video2GUI employs a coarse-to-fine filtering strategy to identify high-quality GUI tutorial videos and convert them into structured agent trajectories. Applying this pipeline to 500 million video metadata entries, we construct WildGUI, a large-scale dataset containing 12 million interaction trajectories spanning over 1,500 applications and websites. Pre-training Qwen2.5-VL and Mimo-VL on WildGUI yields consistent improvements of 5-20% across multiple GUI grounding and action benchmarks, matching or surpassing state-of-the-art performance. We will release both the WildGUI dataset and the Video2GUI pipeline to support future research of GUI agents.

ru.title: Извлечение знаний о GUI-действиях из видео для обучения интеллектуальных агентов
ru.desc: В статье предложена автоматическая система Video2GUI для извлечения траекторий взаимодействия с графическим интерфейсом из видеороликов в интернете без ручных аннотаций. На основе этого подхода создан большой датасет WildGUI, содержащий 12 миллионов траекторий действий пользователей из более чем 1500 приложений и веб-сайтов. Предварительное обучение мультимодальных LLM моделей на этом датасете показало улучшение производительности на 5-20% по задачам понимания GUI и предсказания действий агента. Авторы планируют открыть доступ к датасету и фреймворку для развития методов обучения агентов взаимодействия с пользовательскими интерфейсами.

en.title: Unlocking GUI Agent Potential with WildGUI Dataset
en.desc: This paper presents Video2GUI, an automated framework that extracts interaction trajectories from unlabeled internet videos to create a large-scale dataset for training GUI agents. The resulting dataset, named WildGUI, contains 12 million interaction trajectories from over 1,500 applications, addressing the lack of diverse training data in this field. By pre-training models like Qwen2.5-VL and Mimo-VL on WildGUI, the authors demonstrate significant performance improvements in GUI grounding and action tasks. This work not only enhances the capabilities of GUI agents but also provides valuable resources for future research in this area.

zh.title: 自动化提取，提升GUI代理性能
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为Video2GUI的自动化框架，能够从未标记的互联网视频中提取图形用户界面（GUI）交互轨迹。通过对500百万个视频元数据的处理，构建了一个包含1200万条交互轨迹的大规模数据集WildGUI，涵盖1500多个应用和网站。预训练模型Qwen2.5-VL和Mimo-VL在WildGUI上进行训练后，在多个GUI基础和动作基准测试中表现出5-20%的一致性提升，达到了或超过了当前最先进的性能。该研究为GUI代理的未来研究提供了重要的数据支持和工具。

papers.title: IndusAgent: Reinforcing Open-Vocabulary Industrial Anomaly Detection with Agentic Tools
papers.abstract: Abstract IndusAgent is a tool-augmented agentic framework for open-vocabulary industrial anomaly detection that improves performance through structured visual reasoning and dynamic tool utilization.  AI-generated summary Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have shown remarkable capability in bridging visual perception and textual reasoning, enabling zero-shot understanding across diverse industrial scenarios. However, their performance in open-vocabulary industrial anomaly detection (IAD) is often limited by domain-misaligned reasoning and hallucinated structural inferences. To address these challenges, we propose IndusAgent, a tool-augmented agentic framework for open-vocabulary IAD. Specifically, we first construct Indus-CoT, a structured dataset that integrates global visual observations, high-resolution local patches, and expert normalcy priors, providing supervision for fine-tuning the model on rigorous industrial inspection trajectories. Building on this, IndusAgent dynamically orchestrates a set of external tools, including dynamic region cropping, high-frequency feature enhancement, and prior retrieval, thus enabling the agent to actively resolve visual ambiguities and disentangle subtle anomalies. Furthermore, we introduce a gated reinforcement learning objective that jointly optimizes anomaly classification, localization accuracy, anomaly type reasoning, and efficient tool usage, ensuring that tool invocation occurs only when beneficial. Extensive evaluations on five industrial anomaly benchmarks, including MVTec-AD, VisA, MPDD, DTD, and SDD, demonstrate that IndusAgent achieves state-of-the-art zero-shot performance among all existing methods, validating our robustness and generalization capacity.
affiliations: State Key Lab of Processors, Institute of Computing Technology, CAS
University of Chinese Academy of Sciences, Beijing, China

ru.title: Агентная система с динамическими инструментами для промышленного обнаружения аномалий
ru.desc: IndusAgent — это агентная система на основе мультимодальных больших языковых моделей для обнаружения аномалий в промышленности с открытым словарём. Система использует структурированный набор данных Indus-CoT, содержащий глобальные наблюдения, локальные детали высокого разрешения и экспертные знания о нормальном состоянии. Агент динамически применяет инструменты для разрешения визуальной неопределённости, включая адаптивное кадрирование областей и усиление высокочастотных признаков. Обучение оптимизируется с помощью reinforcement learning, балансируя точность классификации, локализацию аномалий и эффективное использование инструментов.

en.title: Revolutionizing Industrial Anomaly Detection with IndusAgent
en.desc: IndusAgent is a novel framework designed for detecting anomalies in industrial settings using open-vocabulary approaches. It enhances performance by integrating structured visual reasoning with dynamic tool utilization, allowing for better handling of visual ambiguities. The framework utilizes a specially constructed dataset, Indus-CoT, which combines various visual observations and expert knowledge to improve model training. Additionally, it employs a gated reinforcement learning strategy to optimize the use of tools, ensuring that they are activated only when they provide a clear benefit, leading to superior anomaly detection results across multiple benchmarks.

zh.title: 工业异常检测的新工具：IndusAgent
zh.desc: IndusAgent 是一个增强工具的框架，用于开放词汇的工业异常检测。它通过结构化的视觉推理和动态工具利用来提高性能。我们构建了 Indus-CoT 数据集，整合了全球视觉观察和高分辨率局部图像，以支持模型的精细调优。IndusAgent 还引入了门控强化学习目标，优化异常分类和定位精度，确保工具的有效使用。

papers.title: Generative Recursive Reasoning
papers.abstract: Abstract Generative Recursive reAsoning Models (GRAM) introduce probabilistic multi-trajectory computation for neural reasoning systems, enabling multiple hypotheses and parallel inference through stochastic latent trajectories.  AI-generated summary How should future neural reasoning systems implement extended computation? Recursive Reasoning Models (RRMs) offer a promising alternative to autoregressive sequence extension by performing iterative latent-state refinement with shared transition functions. Yet existing RRMs are largely deterministic, following a single latent trajectory and converging to a single prediction. We introduce Generative Recursive reAsoning Models (GRAM), a framework that turns recursive latent reasoning into probabilistic multi-trajectory computation. GRAM models reasoning as a stochastic latent trajectory, enabling multiple hypotheses, alternative solution strategies, and inference-time scaling through both recursive depth and parallel trajectory sampling. This yields a latent-variable generative model supporting conditional reasoning via p_θ(y mid x) and, with fixed or absent inputs, unconditional generation via p_θ(x). Trained with amortized variational inference, GRAM improves over deterministic recurrent and recursive baselines on structured reasoning and multi-solution constraint satisfaction tasks, while demonstrating an unconditional generation capability. https://ahn-ml.github.io/gram-website

ru.title: Вероятностные траектории для многовариантного рассуждения в нейронных сетях
ru.desc: В статье представлена модель GRAM (Generative Recursive reAsoning Models), которая расширяет рекурсивные модели рассуждений, добавляя стохастичность в вычисления скрытых состояний. Вместо следования одной детерминированной траектории, модель исследует множество вероятностных путей одновременно, позволяя системе генерировать несколько гипотез и применять различные стратегии решения. GRAM обучается с помощью вариационного вывода и поддерживает как условную генерацию (предсказание выходов по входам), так и безусловную генерацию. Экспериментально показано, что модель превосходит детерминированные рекурсивные базовые линии на задачах структурированного рассуждения и поиска множественных решений в задачах удовлетворения ограничений.

en.title: Unlocking Multi-Hypothesis Reasoning with GRAM
en.desc: Generative Recursive reAsoning Models (GRAM) enhance neural reasoning systems by introducing probabilistic multi-trajectory computation. This allows the model to explore multiple hypotheses and perform parallel inference, rather than following a single deterministic path. By utilizing stochastic latent trajectories, GRAM supports both conditional and unconditional reasoning, improving the model's flexibility and performance. The framework is trained using amortized variational inference, showing significant advancements in structured reasoning tasks compared to traditional methods.

zh.title: 生成递归推理模型：多轨迹推理的新视角
zh.desc: 生成递归推理模型（GRAM）引入了概率多轨迹计算，增强了神经推理系统的能力。它通过随机潜在轨迹支持多个假设和并行推理，克服了传统递归推理模型的确定性限制。GRAM将推理视为随机潜在轨迹，使得在推理过程中可以进行深度递归和轨迹采样，从而实现条件推理和无条件生成。经过变分推断训练，GRAM在结构化推理和多解约束满足任务上表现优于传统模型。

papers.title: CutVerse: A Compositional GUI Agents Benchmark for Media Post-Production Editing
papers.abstract: Abstract Current GUI agents show limited effectiveness in professional media post-production tasks despite advances in spatial grounding and multimodal alignment.  AI-generated summary While GUI agents have made significant progress in web navigation and basic operating system tasks, their capabilities in professional creative workflows remain largely underexplored. To bridge this gap, we introduce Cutverse, a benchmark designed to systematically evaluate autonomous GUI agents in realistic media post-production environments. We curate expert demonstrations across 7 professional applications (e.g., Premiere Pro, Photoshop), covering 186 complex, long-horizon tasks grounded in authentic editing workflows, involving dense multimodal interfaces and tightly coupled interaction sequences. To support scalable evaluation, we develop a lightweight parser that transforms raw screen recordings and low-level interaction logs into structured, compositional GUI action trajectories with precise grounding. Extensive evaluations reveal that existing agents achieve only 36.0\% task success on realistic media editing tasks, underscoring the challenges posed by complex, long-horizon media post-production workflows in our benchmark.While current models demonstrate promising spatial grounding, multimodal alignment, and coordinated action execution, they remain limited in long-horizon reliability and domain-specific planning.

ru.title: Когда ИИ учится редактировать видео как профессионал
ru.desc: Статья представляет Cutverse — бенчмарк для оценки автономных GUI агентов в задачах профессионального видео- и фото-редактирования. Авторы собрали датасет из 186 сложных, долгоизвестных задач, снятых в реальных приложениях (Premiere Pro, Photoshop и др.), с плотными мультимодальными интерфейсами и связанными последовательностями действий. Они разработали парсер для преобразования записей экрана в структурированные траектории действий GUI с точной привязкой к координатам. Результаты показали, что существующие модели достигают только 36% успеха в решении реальных задач редактирования, выявляя существенные ограничения в долгоизвестном планировании и надёжности современных агентов.

en.title: Bridging the Gap in Media Post-Production with Cutverse
en.desc: This paper discusses the limitations of current GUI agents in handling complex media post-production tasks, despite advancements in spatial grounding and multimodal alignment. The authors introduce Cutverse, a benchmark that evaluates these agents in realistic editing environments, using expert demonstrations from various professional applications. They developed a parser to convert raw interaction data into structured action trajectories, allowing for scalable evaluation of agent performance. The findings indicate that existing agents struggle with long-horizon tasks, achieving only 36.0% success, highlighting the need for improved planning and reliability in creative workflows.

zh.title: 提升GUI代理在媒体后期制作中的表现
zh.desc: 当前的图形用户界面（GUI）代理在专业媒体后期制作任务中的效果有限，尽管在空间定位和多模态对齐方面有所进展。为了填补这一空白，我们提出了Cutverse，这是一个基准测试，旨在系统地评估自主GUI代理在真实媒体后期制作环境中的表现。我们收集了7个专业应用程序（如Premiere Pro和Photoshop）中的专家演示，涵盖186个复杂的长期任务，涉及密集的多模态界面和紧密耦合的交互序列。评估结果显示，现有代理在真实媒体编辑任务中的成功率仅为36.0%，突显了复杂的长期媒体后期制作工作流程所带来的挑战。

papers.title: Evaluating Temporal Semantic Caching and Workflow Optimization in Agentic Plan-Execute Pipelines
papers.abstract: Abstract Industrial asset operations workflows face latency challenges due to complex coordination needs, addressed through novel caching and workflow optimization techniques that improve execution speed while maintaining correctness in parameter-rich environments.  AI-generated summary Industrial asset operations workflows are latency-sensitive because a single user query may require coordination over sensor data, work orders, failure modes, forecasting tools, and domain-specific agents. We evaluate this problem on AssetOpsBench (AOB), an industrial agent benchmark whose plan-execute pipeline exposes repeated overhead from tool discovery, LLM planning, MCP tool execution, and final summarization. Existing LLM caching techniques such as KV-cache reuse and embedding-based semantic caching were designed for chatbot serving and break down when output validity depends on time, asset, or sensor parameters. We propose two complementary optimization layers for AOB plan-execute pipelines: a temporal semantic cache and a set of MCP workflow optimizations combining disk-backed tool-discovery caching and dependency-aware parallel step execution. MCP workflow optimizations corresponded to a 1.67x speedup and reduced median end-to-end latency by about 40.0% while the temporal-cache benchmark achieved a median of 30.6x speedup on cache hits. Beyond the speedup, our results expose a concrete failure mode of pure semantic caching for parameter-rich industrial queries, providing a critical analysis of how caching choices interact with evaluation correctness in MCP-backed agent benchmarks.

ru.title: Ускорение промышленных агент-систем через адаптивное кэширование и оптимизацию рабочих процессов
ru.desc: В статье рассматривается проблема высокой задержки при выполнении операционных рабочих процессов в промышленности, где одной задачи требуется координация между множеством инструментов и агентов. Авторы предлагают два дополняющих друг друга подхода оптимизации: временный семантический кэш и оптимизации рабочего процесса MCP, включающие кэширование обнаружения инструментов и параллельное выполнение зависимых шагов. Эксперименты показали, что эти техники достигают ускорения в 1.67x и 30.6x соответственно при сохранении корректности результатов в параметр-зависимых сценариях. Работа также раскрывает критические ограничения существующих методов семантического кэширования для промышленных запросов с большим числом параметров.

en.title: Optimizing Latency in Industrial Workflows with Smart Caching
en.desc: This paper addresses latency issues in industrial asset operations workflows, which require coordination across various data sources and tools. The authors evaluate these challenges using AssetOpsBench (AOB), highlighting inefficiencies in existing LLM caching methods when dealing with time-sensitive and parameter-rich queries. They propose two optimization strategies: a temporal semantic cache and MCP workflow enhancements, which significantly improve execution speed and reduce latency. The results demonstrate a notable speedup in processing while also revealing limitations of traditional caching approaches in complex industrial environments.

zh.title: 提升工业资产操作效率的创新优化方案
zh.desc: 本论文探讨了工业资产操作工作流程中的延迟问题，提出了新颖的缓存和工作流程优化技术，以提高执行速度并保持参数丰富环境中的正确性。研究表明，单个用户查询可能需要协调多个数据源，因此延迟敏感。我们在AssetOpsBench（AOB）基准上评估了这一问题，发现现有的LLM缓存技术在处理时间、资产或传感器参数时存在局限性。我们提出了两层互补的优化方案，显著提高了工作流程的执行效率，减少了延迟，同时揭示了纯语义缓存的具体失败模式。

papers.title: On the limits and opportunities of AI reviewers: Reviewing the reviews of Nature-family papers with 45 expert scientists
papers.abstract: Abstract AI reviewers demonstrate superior performance in identifying correct criticisms compared to human reviewers, yet exhibit limitations in subfield knowledge and context management that distinguish them from human peers.  AI-generated summary With the advancement of AI capabilities, AI reviewers are beginning to be deployed in scientific peer review, yet their capability and credibility remain in question: many scientists simply view them as probabilistic systems without the expertise to evaluate research, while other researchers are more optimistic about their readiness without concrete evidence. Understanding what AI reviewers do well, where they fall short, and what challenges remain is essential. However, existing evaluations of AI reviewers have focused on whether their verdicts match human verdicts (e.g., score alignment, acceptance prediction), which is insufficient to characterize their capabilities and limits. In this paper, we close this gap through a large-scale expert annotation study, in which 45 domain scientists in Physical, Biological, and Health Sciences spent 469 hours rating 2,960 individual criticisms (each targeting one specific aspect of a paper) from human-written and AI-generated reviews of 82 Nature-family papers on correctness, significance, and sufficiency of evidence. On a composite of all three dimensions, a reviewing agent powered by GPT-5.2 scores above each paper's top-rated human reviewer (60.0% vs. 48.2%, p = 0.009), while all three AI reviewers (including Gemini 3.0 Pro and Claude Opus 4.5) exceed the lowest-rated human across every dimension. AI reviewers' accurate criticisms are also more often rated significant and well-evidenced, and surface a distinct 26% of issues no human raises. However, AI reviewers overlap far more than humans do (21% vs. 3% for cross-reviewer pairs), and exhibit 16 recurring weaknesses humans do not share, such as limited subfield knowledge, lack of long context management over multiple files, and overly critical stance on minor issues. Overall, our results position current AI reviewers as complements to, not substitutes for, human reviewers.
